title: Check Food Expiration
summary: Check expiration dates and know when food is no longer safe to eat.
learnerPromise: You will be able to read date labels on food and know when an item is likely no longer safe to eat.
whyItMatters: Understanding food dates helps you avoid eating spoiled food and reduce waste by using items while they are still good. It is a simple way to protect your health.
realLifeUses: Checking milk before pouring it on cereal
Deciding whether leftover takeout is still good
Sorting through pantry items before shopping
Checking packaged meat before cooking dinner
materialsNeeded: Food packages from your fridge or pantry
result: You can check dates and signs of spoilage and decide if food is still usable.
youWillLearn: Read and understand 'best by,' 'sell by,' and 'use by' dates
Check for signs of spoilage like smell, color, or texture changes
Know which foods are risky to eat past their date
Organize your fridge and pantry so older items get used first
miniChallenge: Go through your fridge and pantry. Check five items for their date labels. Set aside anything that is past date or shows signs of spoilage.
steps: Pick five items from your fridge or pantry and find the date printed on each.
Learn the three common labels: 'best by' means quality, 'sell by' is for stores, and 'use by' is the safety date.
Look at the food itself. Check for mold, odd smells, or color changes.
Throw away anything that smells bad, looks wrong, or is past its 'use by' date.
Move older items to the front of your fridge or shelf so they get used first.
completionCriteria: You checked at least five items for date labels
You identified at least one item to use soon or throw away
You can explain the difference between 'best by' and 'use by'
commonProblems: Confusing 'best by' with a safety date and throwing out good food
Not checking food that looks fine but smells off
Storing new items in front of old ones, so older food gets forgotten
tips: When in doubt, throw it out. It is better to waste a little food than risk getting sick.
Write the date you opened a package on the container with a marker.
Most canned goods last well past the 'best by' date if the can is not bulging or damaged.
